extraction.one_line_summary: MODOLO Sacha won stage 2 Tour de Pologne by finding gaps in chaotic final kilometer sprint after final roundabout destroyed team leadouts
tactical_patterns: Sky committed four riders to Poppel leadout but final roundabout at 1km destroyed organization, leaving messy sprint
Seven-rider team format explicitly noted as creating leadout chaos compared to standard eight-man squads for sprint control
Modolo won by freelancing left through gaps from 500m, while structured leadouts collapsed in narrow technical approach

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: boxed on left side at 500m, attempted right-hand gap that didn't exist, finished only 8th despite jersey
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: mental fragility noted when leadout fails early, needs high-speed launch specifically or loses confidence and quits
rider_signals.POPPEL Danny: positive: took race lead with second place despite Sky's four-man leadout collapsing, shows form improvement
rider_signals.MODOLO Sacha: positive: first WorldTour win in two years exploiting messy sprints, historically excels when organization breaks down
rider_signals.HAN Nathan: neutral: third consecutive stage near front in finale but no result, persistent positioning suggests form
